headline.purpose: Page banner titles only. Overlaid on banner images.

title.purpose: Section headings inside cards and panels.

label.purpose: Tags, form labels, table headers, badge text, metadata.

shadows.name: flat
shadows.purpose: All resting data cards, section panels, table containers. Default state.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Interactive elements at rest: user chip, explicitly interactive cards.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Modals and dialogs. Communicates the element floats above the full page layer.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Toast notifications, dropdown menus, user chip on hover.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Button hover/active, nav item transitions, icon color changes.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Sidebar collapse/expand, modal entry, dropdown open.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Standard easing for state transitions. Elements decelerate into their final position.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main action trigger. Alert Crimson background. Used for respond, save, submit, confirm actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #C20000; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: "Funnel Sans", Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #A10000; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #800000; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C20000; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary and cancel actions. Transparent with subtle border. Pairs with the primary button in any action group.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: #4B5563; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: "Funnel Sans", Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #F9FAFB; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C20000; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-outline:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Primary grouping surface. Flat at rest. White background against gray-50 body is sufficient depth signal.
components.html:
Blood Stock Overview

Current vs ideal units across the hospital network. Updated every 15 minutes.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #F3F4F6; border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px; font-family: "Funnel Sans", Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: #1F2937; line-height: 1.4; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 13px; color: #6B7280; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; }

components.name: Priority Badges
components.description: Severity labels on alerts, requests, and notifications. Sentence-case text. Color + text redundancy always.
components.html: Critical riskHigh riskMedium riskLow risk
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: "Funnel Sans", Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.25; } .ds-badge-critical { background: #FEE2E2; color: #B91C1C; } .ds-badge-high { background: #FFEDD5; color: #C2410C; } .ds-badge-medium { background: #FEF9C3; color: #A16207; } .ds-badge-low { background: #DCFCE7; color: #15803D; }

components.name: Alert Card
components.description: Time-sensitive blood supply notification surface. Always shows priority badge, icon, title, and message. Dismissible.
components.html:
Critical riskO- stock at SGH critical

Stock at 18% of ideal. Immediate restocking required.

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: KPI summary tile. Icon well, large value, optional label and action link. Highlight variant uses Alert Crimson for the value.
components.html:
Blood Units
85%
Of ideal
components.css: .ds-stat-card { display:flex;gap:12px;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #F3F4F6;border-radius:12px;padding:16px;font-family:"Funnel Sans",Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; } .ds-stat-icon-wrap { width:40px;height:40px;border-radius:8px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;flex-shrink:0; } .ds-stat-icon-primary { background:#FFDADA;color:#C20000; } .ds-stat-body { min-width:0; } .ds-stat-label { font-size:13px;color:#6B7280;font-weight:500;line-height:1.25; } .ds-stat-value { font-size:24px;font-weight:700;color:#1F2937;margin-top:2px;line-height:1.2; } .ds-stat-highlight { color:#C20000; } .ds-stat-sub { font-size:13px;color:#6B7280;margin-top:2px; } .ds-stat-link { background:none;border:none;padding:0;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px;color:#C20000;font-weight:500;margin-top:4px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:2px;font-family:inherit; } .ds-stat-link:hover { text-decoration:underline; }

components.name: Sidebar Navigation
components.description: Left sidebar nav items showing active (crimson) and default (gray) states.
components.html: HomeDemand ForecastingAlerts4

components.name: Toast Notification
components.description: Floating feedback surface. Fixed top-right. Auto-dismiss at 4s. Four types: success, warning, error, info.
components.html:
Alert dismissed
The alert has been marked as resolved.
components.css: .ds-toast { display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:12px;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #D1D5DB;border-radius:12px;padding:16px;max-width:350px;font-family:"Funnel Sans",Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;box-shadow:0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.10),0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-toast-success { border-color:#BBF7D0; } .ds-toast-icon { width:20px;height:20px;flex-shrink:0;margin-top:1px; } .ds-toast-success .ds-toast-icon { color:#22C55E; } .ds-toast-body { flex:1; } .ds-toast-title { font-size:15px;font-weight:600;color:#15803D;line-height:1.4; } .ds-toast-message { font-size:13px;color:#6B7280;margin-top:2px;line-height:1.4; } .ds-toast-close { background:none;border:none;cursor:pointer;color:#9CA3AF;padding:0;display:flex;align-items:center;flex-shrink:0; } .ds-toast-close:hover { color:#4B5563; }

components.name: Blood Stock Dot Legend
components.description: Signature component. 12x12px status dots for the hospital x blood-type matrix. Legend is non-optional — color is never the only channel.
components.html:
Good (70–100%)
Low (40–69%)
Critical (0–39%)
No data

narrative.overview:
codeRED is a tool built for people in motion: coordinators, analysts, and field staff who carry the weight of national blood supply decisions. The visual system reflects their reality — minimal friction, maximum clarity, and a calm authority that does not add noise to an already demanding job. Nothing decorates. Every element earns its position by doing work.

The palette is deliberately restrained. Alert Crimson (#C20000) is a brand anchor and a severity signal, not a decoration. It appears on primary actions, active navigation states, and critical status indicators. Its rarity is the point: when something is red, users know it means something.

This system explicitly rejects the aesthetic of generic government portals (cluttered, low-contrast, dated form layouts) and consumer health apps (soft pastels, patient-facing wellness tone). codeRED is a professional instrument.
keyCharacteristics: Single-family type system (Funnel Sans) with weight-driven hierarchy — no display face
Alert Crimson used on ≤10% of any given screen
Flat surfaces at rest; depth emerges only through interaction layer (modal, toast, dropdown)
Complete semantic status vocabulary (amber, green, blue, red) used consistently across all surfaces
Status indicators always paired with text labels — color is never the only channel

rules.name: The Alert Crimson Rule
rules.body: Alert Crimson (#C20000) appears on ≤10% of any given screen. It is the accent, not the canvas. Every use is either a primary action, an active state, or a critical severity signal. Using it for decoration is a mistake.

rules.name: The Tint Pairing Rule
rules.body: Every semantic status color (amber, green, blue, red) has a light tint counterpart. Status surfaces always use the light tint as background and the saturated color for icons, text, and borders. Never full-saturation status backgrounds on large surfaces.

rules.name: The Weight-Contrast Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is expressed through weight, not size alone. A title and body text may share the same 15px size; 600 vs 400 weight is sufficient differentiation for dashboard contexts.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Funnel Sans is the only typeface in this system. No decorative display face on dashboard surfaces. This is a tool, and its type should disappear into the task.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadow appears only when an element lifts above the content plane to float (modal, toast, dropdown) or when an element gains interactive lift on hover. A card that bears data but is not interactive has no shadow.

dos: Do use Alert Crimson (#C20000) exclusively for primary actions, active states, and critical severity.
Do pair every color-coded status indicator with a text label. Color alone fails users with color vision deficiencies.
Do keep body text at Ink Primary (#1F2937) on card surfaces. Using Ink Muted for body copy fails WCAG AA contrast.
Do use the semantic status color vocabulary (crimson, amber, green, blue) consistently across all surfaces.
Do write button labels as verb + object: 'Respond to request', 'Dismiss alert', 'View all requests'.
Do use skeleton loading states instead of center-screen spinners inside data panels.
Do assign shadow weight according to layer position: flat for data cards, ambient low for interactive elements, overlay for modals, floating for toasts.
donts: Don't use Alert Crimson as a background color on any surface larger than a badge pill.
Don't design for a generic government portal aesthetic: cluttered layouts, low-contrast type, excessive borders as section dividers.
Don't adopt consumer health app aesthetics: soft pastel palettes, rounded-friendly type, patient-facing wellness tone.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Alert Crimson as a solid color carries more authority.
Don't use a colored border-left or border-right greater than 1px as an accent stripe. Use full borders with tinted backgrounds instead.
Don't add decorative motion. All animation conveys state: loading, entry, dismiss, hover feedback.
Don't invent a new status color outside the four semantic roles (crimson, amber, green, blue).
Don't use the 36px headline size inside content panels — it is reserved for page banner titles only.
